ground_truth.description: Photo from Karl Johan street, Oslo. Royal palace visible in distance (somewhat covered by trees). Side of Stortinget barely visible.
ground_truth.photographer_description: Photo from Karl Johan street, Oslo. You can see the royal palace in the distance, but it is somewhat covered by trees and other buildings. It should still be able to recognize it though. You can also barely see the side of the Stortinget. I don't expect it to recognize it, but impressive if it does. Overall, I expect it to recognize that it's Karl Johan and that it can see the royal palace in the background, even without metadata.

details.description: A busy, cobblestoned stretch of a grand European boulevard lined with flower planters and historic street lamps leads toward a large neoclassical palace framed by trees. On the left is a long yellow-brick institutional building with arched windows and decorative stonework in a Romanesque-revival idiom; on the right are late‑19th/early‑20th‑century facades with shopfronts and awnings. Numerous pedestrians fill the street under soft, partly cloudy daylight. The perspective looks along the tree-lined axis toward the palace at the far end, while the prominent yellow-brick building dominates the foreground left. The scene conveys Oslo’s central ceremonial axis with civic architecture and a lively public realm.

details.description: A wide, sunlit street scene along Karl Johans gate in central Oslo. In the foreground, a cobblestone pedestrian boulevard is flanked by a large, ornate brick building on the left featuring tall arched windows and decorative masonry. To the right, a row of historic pastel-colored buildings with shopfronts and small balconies line the avenue. Classic street lamps with curved arms rise above the sidewalks, and flower planters add bursts of color along the edges. People stroll and converse as the avenue stretches toward the distant horizon. At the far end, a grand neoclassical building with columns and a broad staircase dominates the view, suggesting a prominent civic structure such as the Norwegian Parliament.

details.description: Busy city street scene in central Oslo looking up Karl Johans gate. The cobblestoned boulevard is lined with flower planters, historic lampposts, and crowds of pedestrians. On the left is the long yellow‑brick facade of the Norwegian Parliament building (Stortinget) with repeated round‑arched windows. The street rises toward a tree‑framed neoclassical palace visible in the distance, the Royal Palace at the western end of Karl Johans gate. Historic shopfronts and cafes line the right side under soft, cloudy daylight.

details.description: A bustling pedestrian street scene in central Oslo, viewed along Karl Johans gate. The left foreground features a large, historic brick building with arched windows and decorative masonry, while the right side shows a row of shops and balconies with awnings. The cobblestone street is lined with modern street lamps and flower planters, and numerous people are strolling, chatting, and taking in the city. In the distance, the avenue opens toward a grand, neoclassical building atop a gentle slope, suggesting the Royal Palace vicinity as a focal point. The sky is partly cloudy, casting soft light over the scene.

entities.name: Karl Johans gate
entities.notes: Main street in Oslo from which the photo is taken, leading toward the Royal Palace area.

details.description: A street-level perspective looking west up Karl Johans gate in Oslo, Norway. The foreground features a cobblestone street with a pedestrian crosswalk and people walking away from the camera. To the immediate left stands the imposing yellow brick facade of the Stortinget (Norwegian Parliament) building, characterized by its Romanesque Revival style with tall arched windows and a corner tower. On the right side of the street, historic commercial buildings with light-colored facades line the avenue, some displaying flags and signage (including a red 'SALE' banner). In the middle ground, the trees of Studenterlunden park line the left side of the street. The street leads the eye directly to the background, where the Royal Palace (Det Kongelige Slott) sits atop a hill, centered in the composition. The equestrian statue of King Karl Johan is visible in silhouette in front of the palace. The sky is overcast with soft, diffused light.

details.description: A view looking west down Karl Johans gate, the main street of Oslo, Norway. On the left is the yellow-brick facade of the Stortinget (the Norwegian Parliament building), featuring Romanesque Revival architecture with arched windows and decorative stonework. The street is paved with cobblestones and is filled with pedestrians. In the far distance, at the end of the long, straight vista, sits the Royal Palace (Det kongelige slott), a neoclassical building situated on a slight rise. The scene is captured in daylight with soft, directional light creating long shadows. Trees line the street further down, and classic black street lamps are visible.

details.description: A busy, gently sloping boulevard lined with trees and historic buildings. At the far end of the axis sits a pale yellow neoclassical palace on a hill. In the foreground on the left is a long yellow-brick institutional building with repeated arched windows, and ornate black street lamps with gold finials run down the center of the street. The pavement is cobbled and the area is filled with pedestrians. The overall scene suggests central Oslo, looking along Karl Johans gate from near the Parliament toward the Royal Palace.

details.description: A broad, cobblestone pedestrian boulevard flanked by historic architecture. On the left is a large beige-brick building with arched windows, decorative stonework, and a castellated parapet. The right side features a row of mid-rise shops with awnings and flower displays. Ornate black streetlamps line the street, and people walk along the sidewalks and central promenade. In the distance, a green park area sits beyond a gentle slope, capped by a distant neoclassical building with a columned façade and steps leading upward. The lighting is soft, suggesting late afternoon, with a warm, slightly desaturated tone.

details.description: A lively, cobblestoned boulevard lined with historic buildings and flower planters. On the left is a long yellow-brick structure with repeating arched windows (the Norwegian Parliament building), while shops and cafes line the right. Classic curved street lamps punctuate the scene. In the distance, at the top of the gently rising, tree-lined avenue, stands the pale-yellow Royal Palace of Oslo. Crowds of pedestrians fill the street under a bright, partly cloudy sky—an iconic view along Karl Johans gate toward the palace.

details.description: A European city street scene captured from a pedestrian walkway. A broad cobblestone avenue runs forward between a large, ornate stone building with arched windows on the left and a row of light-colored façades with shop fronts on the right. Decorative street lamps line the street, and people are scattered along the sidewalks and in the distance. Trees form a green corridor along the central area, and planters with flowers add color to the foreground. The street leads to a distant, grand building at the far end, approached by a staircase and flanked by greenery under a partly cloudy sky.

details.description: A street-level vertical photograph looking west up Karl Johans gate in Oslo, Norway. In the immediate foreground, a cobblestone street features a pedestrian zebra crossing where several people are walking, including a man in a blue shirt and white cap standing centrally. To the left stands the imposing yellow-brick facade of the Stortinget (Norwegian Parliament) building, characterized by Neo-Romanesque arched windows and decorative brickwork. On the right, a row of lighter-colored commercial buildings with awnings and signage lines the street. The street slopes gently upward, flanked on the left by the mature trees of the Studenterlunden park. In the center background, at the top of the hill, sits the Royal Palace (Det Kongelige Slott), a pale yellow classical building with a columned portico, fronted by the silhouette of the equestrian statue of King Karl Johan. The sky is overcast with soft, diffused lighting.

details.description: A view looking west down Karl Johans gate, the main street of Oslo, Norway. On the left is the yellow brick facade of the Stortinget (the Norwegian Parliament building), featuring Romanesque Revival architectural elements like arched windows and decorative brickwork. The street is paved with cobblestones and is filled with pedestrians. In the far distance, at the end of the long uphill street, the neoclassical Royal Palace (Slottet) is visible against a cloudy sky. Ornate black street lamps and green trees line the thoroughfare, and several commercial buildings with awnings are visible on the right side of the street.
